untranslatable_reason: Signal requires Kalman filter measurement prediction error e(t) and predicted variance Q(t), which are custom state-space model outputs not available in the standard indicator set. These are derived from a dynamic model estimation process, not basic technical indicators. The signal logic (comparing e(t) to ±sqrt(Q(t))) cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, Volume, Position, Returns, Bar Properties).
translation_notes: This is an advanced mean-reversion signal based on Kalman filtering theory. To implement it, the backtest engine would need to: (1) estimate a state-space model with hidden state and measurement equation, (2) run Kalman filter recursively to compute e(t) = observed - predicted and Q(t) = prediction error variance, (3) compare e(t) against ±sqrt(Q(t)) thresholds. Empirical performance metrics provided: Sharpe=2.4, APR=26.2%.
